segments.text: After this, the Lord appointed seventy other disciples, and sent them in pairs to every town and place that he planned to visit.
The harvest is large, but the number of workers is small,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: he told them.
Pray to the Lord of the harvest to send workers to his harvest fields.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: So get on your way: I'm sending you like sheep among wolves.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Don't take any money or a bag or extra sandals, and don't spend time chatting with people you meet.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Whatever house you enter, first of all say, ‘May this house have peace.’
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: If there's a peaceful person living there, then your peace will rest on them; if not, it will return to you.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Stay in that house, eating and drinking whatever they give you, for a worker deserves to be paid. Don't go from house to house.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: If you enter a town and the people there welcome you, then eat what's set before you
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: and heal those who are sick. Tell them, ‘God's kingdom has come to you.’
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: But if you enter a town and the people there don't welcome you, go through their streets telling them,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: ‘We are wiping off even the dust from your town that clings to our feet to show you our disapproval.  But you should recognize this: God's kingdom has come.’
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: I tell you, in the Day of Judgment it will be better for Sodom than for such a town.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Shame on you Korazin! Shame on you Bethsaida! For if the miracles you saw happen had happened in Tyre and Sidon they would have repented a long time ago, and they would be sitting in sackcloth and ashes.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: That's why in the judgment it will be better for Tyre and Sidon than for you.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: And you, Capernaum, you won't be exalted to heaven; you will go down to Hades.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Anyone who hears you hears me, and anyone who rejects you rejects me. But anyone who rejects me rejects the one who sent me.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: The seventy disciples returned in great excitement, saying, {
Lord, even the demons do what we tell them in your name!
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Jesus replied, {
I saw Satan fall like lightning from heaven.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Yes, I have given you power to tread on snakes and scorpions, and to overcome all the enemy's strength, and nothing will harm you.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But don't take delight that the spirits do what you tell them—just be glad that your names are written in heaven.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: At that moment Jesus was filled with the joy of the Holy Spirit, and said, {
I thank you Father, Lord of heaven and earth, for you hid these things from the wise and clever people and revealed them to children! Yes, Father, you were pleased to do it in this way.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: My Father has handed over everything to me. No one understands the Son except the Father, and no one understands the Father except the Son, and those to whom the Son chooses to reveal him.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: When they were by themselves Jesus turned to the disciples and told them, {
Those who see what you're seeing should be really happy!
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: I tell you, many prophets and kings have wanted to see what you're seeing, but they didn't see, and wanted to hear the things you're hearing, but didn't hear.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Once an expert in religious law stood up and tried to trap Jesus. {
Teacher,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: he asked,
What do I have to do to gain eternal life?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: What is written in the law? How do you read it?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: asked Jesus.
You shall love the Lord your God with your whole heart, and your whole spirit, and your whole strength, and your whole mind; and love your neighbor as yourself,
segments.speaker: the man

segments.text: the man replied.
You're right,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jesus told him.
Do this, and you will live.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But the man wanted to vindicate himself, so he asked Jesus, {
And who is my neighbor?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jesus replied, saying, {
A man was going down from Jerusalem to Jericho. He was attacked by robbers who stripped him and beat him, and left him for dead.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: It so happened that a priest was going the same way. He saw the man, but he passed by on the other side of the road.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Then a Levite came along. But when he got to the place and saw the man, he also passed by on the other side.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Finally a Samaritan man came along. As he passed by, he saw the man and felt sorry for him.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: He went over and treated the man's wounds with oil and wine, and bandaged them. Then he placed the man on his own donkey and took him to an inn where he took care of him.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: The next day he gave two denarii to the innkeeper and told him, ‘Take care of him, and if you spend more than this, I'll pay you back when I return.’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Which one of these three do you think was a neighbor to the man who was attacked by robbers?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: The one who showed him kindness,
segments.speaker: The man

segments.text: the man replied.
You go and do the same,
segments.speaker: The man

segments.text: Jesus told him.
While they were on their way,  Jesus arrived at a village, and a woman called Martha invited him to her home.
She had a sister called Mary, who sat at the Lord's feet and listened to his teaching.
Martha was concerned about all that needed to be done to prepare the meal, so she came to Jesus and said, {
Master, don't you care that my sister has left me to do all the work by myself? Tell her to come and help me!
segments.speaker: The man

segments.text: Martha, Martha,
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: the Lord replied,
you're worried and upset about all this.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: But only one thing is really necessary. Mary has chosen the right thing, and it shall not be taken away from her.
segments.speaker: the Lord
